briefing:
# Atlantic Fintech Daily Briefing
### Monday, March 2, 2026

---

## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News / TechCrunch* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines rather than actual artificial intelligence.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News / CNBC* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of fintech middleware firm Synapse, highlighting the risks of uninsured fintech banking products.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News / FF News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling the tech giant's growing interest in financial data and credit assessment services.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News / Bits About Money* — A deep dive into how open banking frameworks are reshaping payments competition and what that means for consumers and incumbents alike.

- **[Deepfake attack: 'Many people could have been cheated'](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c0j59vydxj9o?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)** — *BBC Business* — The head of the Bombay Stock Exchange was recently targeted in a deepfake scam, underscoring the growing global threat of AI-powered financial fraud.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Canada's economy contracted unexpectedly in fourth quarter of 2025](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/canada-gdp-dec-2025-9.7108015?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada's GDP shrank unexpectedly in Q4 2025, raising concerns about the economic environment facing fintech startups and financial services investment across the country.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News / FinLedger* — Canada's federal government flagged screen scraping as a security risk and outlined its ambitions for a formal open banking framework — a policy journey still unfolding today. *(Included for ongoing relevance to Canadian open banking regulation.)*

- **[China suspending some agricultural tariffs on Canada starting March 1](https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/canada-suspends-tariffs-some-canada-9.7108023?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — China's decision to suspend select agricultural tariffs on Canada signals a potential easing of trade tensions with implications for Canadian business confidence and cross-border payments flows.

- **[Ottawa makes first RDII investments in Ontario with $15 million for CDL Defence and Wolf Advanced Technology](https://betakit.com/ottawa-makes-first-rdii-investments-in-ontario-with-15-million-for-cdl-defence-and-wolf-advanced-technology/)** — *BetaKit* — The federal government deployed its first Ontario-based RDII investments, a signal of continued public appetite for backing Canadian innovation ecosystems — relevant context for Atlantic fintech venture watchers.

- **[Québec government invests $36 million to sustain Mila's AI research](https://betakit.com/quebec-government-invests-36-million-to-sustain-milas-ai-research/)** — *BetaKit* — Québec is doubling down on AI research with a $36 million commitment to Mila, reinforcing Canada's position as a global AI hub with downstream implications for AI-powered fintech development.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — A provocative look at the darker consequences of unchecked fintech growth, offering a timely counterpoint for ecosystem builders in Atlantic Canada focused on responsible and inclusive financial innovation.

- **[Oil jumps 10% on Iran conflict and could spike to $100 US a barrel, analysts say](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/calgary/oil-jumps-10-on-iran-conflict-and-could-spike-to-100-us-a-barrel-analysts-say-9.7110557?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Surging oil prices driven by Middle East conflict could significantly impact Newfoundland & Labrador's offshore energy revenues and the broader Atlantic Canadian economic landscape that underpins regional fintech demand.

- **[US Treasuries Fall as Inflation Angst Eclipses Haven Buying](https://financialpost.com/pmn/business-pmn/us-treasuries-fall-as-inflation-angst-eclipses-haven-buying)** — *Financial Post* — Rising inflation fears tied to oil price spikes are rattling bond markets, a macro signal that Atlantic Canadian credit unions and fintech lenders should watch closely as interest rate expectations shift.

---

*📬 Curated by Atlantic Fintech | Growing the fintech ecosystem in Nova Scotia, New Brunswick, PEI & Newfoundland & Labrador.*
